creator: scheidell@FreeBSD.org
text:
Thank you for your work on FreeBSD ports, and updating this to work with the new PHP 5.4 default. Not sure how you attached the patch, but it was not attached as a plain text file. --e89a8f3ba417eccede04c0718f8a Content-Type: text/html; charset=UTF-8 Update PHP 5.4 (sqlite2)
Attached file
--e89a8f3ba417eccede04c0718f8a-- --e89a8f3ba417eccee604c0718f8c Content-Type: application/octet-stream; name="sqlite2_php54.patch" Content-Disposition: attachment; filename="sqlite2_php54.patch" Content-Transfer-Encoding: base64 X-Attachment-Id: f_h2fpb3o01 please either close this and reopen a new pr, or update pr with a new patch, attached as a plain text attachment (not inline email, but plain text attachment) (hint: if you used command line, send-pr, you create the patch and then: send-pr -a {patchfile} Category needs to be 'ports' subject should include databases/php5-sqlite so that maintainer is notified. -- Michael Scheidell, CTO >*| * SECNAP Network Security Corporation d: +1.561.948.2259 w: http://people.freebsd.org/~scheidell
